'''Taulima Tautai''' born 3 April 1988<ref name="parraeels">{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display&id=5209&player=taulima-tautai&team=Eels|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120322055454/http://www.parraeels.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display&id=5209&player=taulima-tautai&team=Eels|archive-date=22 March 2012|url-status=dead|title=Taulima Tautai - Parramatta Eels|accessdate=28 May 2014}}</ref> is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer, who has most recently played as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], [[Parramatta Eels]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].

==Background==
Born in [[Sydney, New South Wales]], Australia. Tautai attended [[St Gregory's College, Campbelltown]].<ref name="whereilive">{{cite web|url=http://macarthur-chronicle-campbelltown.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/former-leumeah-high-student-jarryd-hayne-to-star-in-nrl-grand-final/|title=Macarthur Chronicle - News Local Newspaper - Daily Telegraph|website=macarthur-chronicle-campbelltown.whereilive.com.au|accessdate=18 August 2017}}</ref>

Taulima played his junior rugby league for CVD Edensor Park Cobras, before being signed by the [[Parramatta Eels]].

==Playing career==
Tautai's position of choice was originally in the {{rlp|ce}}s or on the {{rlp|wg}}.

He made his first-grade début for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in round 1 of the [[2008 NRL season]], going on to play 30 games for the club between 2008 and 2009. Tautai won Parramatta Eels' rookie of the season award in 2008.{{cn|date=June 2025}}

Although born in Sydney, Tautai is of Samoan and New Zealand descent. Because of his heritage, he was named in the [[Samoa national rugby league team#2008 World Cup squad|Samoan]]<ref name="Samoa name World Cup Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750|title=Samoa name World Cup Squad|publisher=League Unlimited|date=5 August 2008|accessdate=6 August 2008|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110717060051/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750|archivedate=17 July 2011|df=dmy-all}}</ref> and [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squads]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup]].<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=8 August 2008|accessdate=8 August 2008}}</ref> However, he suffered an injury and did not make the final squad for either nation.{{cn|date=June 2025}}

Tautai played 33 games for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] between 2010 and 2011.

He returned to the [[Parramatta Eels]] in 2012 but injury and form kept him to only 9 appearances. Tautai was part of the Parramatta side that finished last in the competition that year which was the first time the club had finished last since 1972.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ny0xNTMwLTQtLS0tLS03NDI4LS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLQ==|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=Rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=18 August 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2012/10/02/season-review-parramatta-eels/|title=Season review: Parramatta Eels|website=www.nrl.com|date=2 October 2012 }}</ref>

Tautai joined the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]] for the 2013 and 2014 seasons, playing in 22 matches.

Tautai joined the [[Wigan Warriors]] in November 2014, signing a three-year deal.<ref name="bbc">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27607793|title=BBC Sport - Wigan Warriors: Taulima Tautai to join from Wakefield in 2015|work=BBC Sport |access-date=28 May 2014}}</ref>

He played from the bench in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

In 2018, he played over 20 matches for the season, but was not selected in the finals series, and was not a part of the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] final winning side.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37550948|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington Wolves 6-12 Wigan Warriors|work=BBC Sport |date=8 October 2016|publisher=}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-crowned-2018-super-league-champions/|title=Wigan crowned 2018 Super League champions|first=Drew|last=Darbyshire|date=13 October 2018|publisher=}}</ref>
Tautai left the [[Wigan Warriors]] in June 2019 following a drink-driving conviction.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48756479|title=Wigan Warriors: Taulima Tautai leaves after drink-driving conviction|work=BBC Sport |date=25 June 2019|publisher=}}</ref>

Whilst Tautai was known mostly for his Rugby career, he took place in charity and community work off the field. He donated his 2016 Grand Final ring to a local Wigan family to assist them in raising money for the SANDs charity. A Charity that looks after family who have lost their babies.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/news/mum-stunned-wigan-warriors-stars-generous-gesture-567261|title=Mum stunned by Wigan Warriors star's generous gesture | Wigan Today|accessdate=28 March 2023}}</ref> Alongside his wife, they started the Wigan Warriors Campaign 'Respect Her' after working with the Women's Refuge to draw attention to issues within the local community.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://great-w-bet.com/wigan-prop-taulima-tautai-turns-attention-from-super-league-pitch-to-domestic-violence/ |title=Wigan prop Taulima Tautai turns attention from super league pitch to domestic violence |access-date=20 February 2020 |archive-date=20 February 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200220044833/https://great-w-bet.com/wigan-prop-taulima-tautai-turns-attention-from-super-league-pitch-to-domestic-violence/ |url-status=dead }}</ref> Tautai also shaved off his long locks to raise money for suicide awareness in honour of his mother-in-law 'The Jenny Project'.<ref>{{cite tweet |author=Wigan Warriors Community Foundation 🍒⚪️ |user=WiganWarriorsCF |number=1056864845113516032 |date=29 October 2018 |title=Please donate what you can to support T H E J E N N Y P R O J E C T https://t.co/PLdcZNdcZc… Taulima Tautai is shaving his head to help raise funds for this very special cause #inspiring SHAVE IT OFF Yes, I am shaving my head and donating my hair. https://t.co/PLdcZNdcZc… https://t.co/hJGEUNvTkb |language=en |access-date=16 January 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220910202621/https://twitter.com/wiganwarriorscf/status/1056864845113516032 |archive-date=10 September 2022 |url-status=live}}</ref>

On 30 June 2019, he announced that he would be retiring from rugby league.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/departing-wigan-warriors-star-taulima-tautai-to-retire-from-rugby-league-1-9848848|title=Departing Wigan Warriors star Taulima Tautai to retire from rugby league|website=www.wigantoday.net}}</ref>
